FOR DEFENDANT AMAZON.COM, INC. -- DEFENDANT AMAZON.COM INC.'S JOINT MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL AND ALL RELATED DATES IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 5/28/24 IS CONTINUED TO 9/16/24 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. THERE SHALL BE NO FURTHER CONTINUANCES OF THE TRIAL DATE. ALL DISCOVERY, MOTION AND PRE-TRIAL DEADLINES WILL BE CALCULATED BASED ON THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED. ALL PARTIES MUST EMAIL ADRCOORDINATOR@SFTC.ORG NO LATER THAN SIX WEEKS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE TO SET A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT. PARTIES ALSO MAY REACH OUT VIA EMAIL TO DEPARTMENT206@SFTC.ORG IF THEY WANT A SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE TO BE CONDUCTED BY JUDGE MASSULLO. JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO; CLERK: JIROQUE; COURT REPORTER: MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-22-2026 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL's Motion For Approval Of Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds that the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code, sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law on the evidence before the court; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code, section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. The parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature.

Proposed order and proposed Consent Judgment submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
